title: cacalia microcephala
intro: Cacalia microcephala, also known as the small-headed marsh bellflower, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cacalia microcephala include reducing fever and pain, treating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent.
